title: 2023 Administrative Assistants Exam
subtitle: Production JSON conversion of the source markdown paper
instructions: Question wording and options are preserved from the source markdown.
Progress is saved locally on this device.
Figure placeholders are used where the source markdown did not include actual figure images.

metadata.notes: Direct syllabus question. Tests finding intersection points.

questions.text: The graphs of the linear equations 3x − y + 5 = 0 and 2x + 3y − 4 = 0 intersect at p(α, β). Point p lies on the graph of which of the following equations?

metadata.notes: Combination. Uses Heron's formula and circumradius relation (R=abc/4A).

questions.text: The length of the sides of a triangle are 48 cm, 55 cm and 73 cm. It is inscribed in a circle. The area (in cm²) of the circle excluding the area of the triangle is closest to (use π = 22/7):

metadata.notes: Direct. Trick: Calculation using relative percentages instead of absolute values.

questions.text: By selling an article at 68.75% of its selling price, a shopkeeper loses 12%. If he sells it at 5% less than its original selling price, then what will be his profit per cent?

The monthly expenditure of Tunisha is ₹30,400 which is 20% less than her monthly income. If her monthly income increases by 25% and monthly savings increase by 30%, then the difference between her new monthly expenditure and the initial monthly expenditure is:

metadata.notes: Combination. Combines efficiency ratio with combined work rate.

questions.text: A and B together can complete a certain work in 30 days and B is 20% more efficient than C. A, B and C together can do the same work in 20 days. A alone can do 1/3rd of the original work in:

metadata.notes: Direct. Trick: Finding difference of numbers to determine the divisor.

questions.text: When 2998, 3850 and 5341 are divided by a 2-digit number x, the remainder in each case is y. What is the value of (x − y)?
A certain sum invested at simple interest amounts to ₹6,214 in 3 years and amounts to ₹7,566 in 7 years. What is the rate of interest per annum?

metadata.notes: Direct. Trick: Wordplay and forming simultaneous equations.

questions.text: Three times the present age (in years) of Sampat is one year more than twice the present age of Babita. After 5 years from now, the age of Babita will be 2 years less than twice the present age of Sampat. What is the difference (in years) between the present ages of Babita and Sampat?

metadata.notes: Direct. Careful calculation required to avoid basic errors.
Combination. Tests percentage changes applied to multiple quantities.

questions.text: The cost of a computer is 140% more than the cost of a mobile phone. If the cost of a computer decreases by 20% and that of a mobile phone increases by 40%, then the total cost of 5 computers and 4 mobile phones is x% less than their total initial cost. What is the value of x?

metadata.notes: Direct extension of Time & Work with negative efficiency (emptying).

questions.text: Pipes A and B can fill a tank in 15 hours and 25 hours, respectively. Pipe C is an emptying pipe. When all the three pipes are opened together, then 16% of the tank is filled up in 6 hours. Pipe C alone can empty 36% part of the tank in:

metadata.topic: Right Circular Cone / Time and distance
metadata.notes: Combination. Heavy calculation involving volume of a frustum and speed of water.

questions.text: Water is flowing at the rate of 2 m/sec through a pipe of internal radius 2½ cm into an empty tank, in the shape of a frustum of a cone, with radii of circular ends as 28 cm and 7 cm and height 45 cm. If the tank is completely filled in x seconds, then the value of x is between:

metadata.notes: Combination. Standard relationship between CP, MP, SP, Discount, and Profit.

questions.text: A trader sells an article for ₹462, after giving a discount of 12% on its marked price. Had he not given the discount, he would have earned a profit of 20%. What is the cost price (in ₹) of the article?

metadata.notes: Direct. Accounts for changing investments over time.

questions.text: A and B enter into a partnership with their capitals in the ratio 1/4 : 5/12. After 4 months, A doubles his capital and B reduces his capital by 40%. At the end of a year, what is the share (in ₹ lakhs) of B in the total profit of ₹31.2 lakhs?

metadata.notes: Direct. Setting up upstream/downstream speed equations.

questions.text: The time taken by a boat to go 25 km upstream is 1⅔ times the time taken by it to go 20 km downstream. The speed of the stream is 4 km/h. How much time (in hours) will the boat take to go 70 km in still water?

metadata.notes: Combination. Weighted mixing of two ratios to form a new ratio.

questions.text: In alloys A and B, the ratio of copper and zinc is 3:7 and 4:11, respectively. 2 kg of A and 3 kg of B are melted and mixed to form a new alloy C. What is the percentage of zinc in C?

metadata.notes: Relies on finding logical transitions (But, However).

questions.text:
Sentences of a paragraph are given below in jumbled order. Arrange the sentences in the correct order to form a meaningful and coherent paragraph.
A. But all the money soon vanished into the bottomless pit of household needs.
B. Little did she know that I was out of a job once again.
C. However, my grandmother was happy that I had become responsible.
D. I returned home with pockets full of my earnings.

Sentences of a paragraph are given below in jumbled order. Arrange the sentences in the correct order to form a meaningful and coherent paragraph.
A. These sticks were fuzzy at one end.
B. The fuzzy end was rubbed against the teeth to keep them clean.
C. They used thin twigs called chew sticks as tooth cleaners.
D. How did people clean their teeth before the toothbrush was invented?

Select the most appropriate meaning of the given idiom.
Over the moon

Select the most appropriate meaning of the given idiom.
Hook, line and sinker

Select the option that can be used as a one-word substitute for the given group of words.
A place for burying the dead

Select the most appropriate meaning of the given idiom.
Boil the ocean

Select the most appropriate synonym of the word given in brackets to fill in the blank.
Human life is ______ (ephemeral), we should make every moment worthwhile.

Select the most appropriate option that can substitute the underlined segment in the given sentence. If there is no need to substitute it, select "No substitution required".
He yet bears a grudge against me for not taking him into confidence.
Select the INCORRECTLY spelt word.
Select the correctly spelt word.

Select the most appropriate option that can substitute the underlined segment in the given sentence.
She spent her money judiciously unless she should have to borrow in an emergency.

Select the option that can be used as a one-word substitute for the given group of words.
A particular occupation, business or profession especially suitable for you

Select the most appropriate ANTONYM of the word given in brackets to fill in the blank.
Reema felt ______ (petrified) in the presence of her father although the drive in the hills was very risky.
Which of the following is NOT true about Lawrence of Arabia?

options.text: The film holds a high value in the history of filmmaking.
There is a gradual decline of the protagonist in the film.
The action scenes were performed by real persons.
The characterisation of the protagonist is not convincing.

questions.passage:
The film Lawrence of Arabia may be somewhat dated by modern special-effects standards, but it remains a high-water mark in the annals of filmmaking. Since we're on the subject of computer-generated special effects, Lawrence has none for the simple reason that it was actually filmed using real people who really performed the action. The long, slow scenes of camels walking in the desert may seem dull to the modern animation-jaded viewer, but those willing to pay attention to the underlying themes will be well rewarded by what the movie is saying.

And it is the theme of Lawrence that really stays with a viewer, even today, some 40 years after it was released. That theme is the age-old story of hubris, the pride of a man which raises him above his peers, only to dash him on the rocks of self-indulgence by the end.

We see this tragic foible of mankind worked out fully in the character of Lawrence himself, who begins the film as a brilliant genius who is eager to get involved in the desert conflicts of World War I. His cocky attitude irritates his superiors and amuses the Arabs fighting alongside the British, but his genius for details and strategy soon overcome all obstacles. Lawrence's brilliant victory at the Suez Canal could only have been accomplished by Lawrence, and his own self-sacrifice and commitment to his followers display the best of his character.

Unfortunately, the baser elements of that character gradually take control as the film moves along, and Lawrence slowly declines into a dangerous blend of despair and self-assurance. The problem gradually becomes evident: Lawrence has grown to believe that he is equal to God, the one who both gives life and takes it away again.

metadata.notes: Inference based on overall passage meaning.

questions.text: Which of the following can be described as the theme of the film?

options.text: All that glitters is not gold.
A stitch in time saves nine.

questions.text: "The film Lawrence of Arabia may be somewhat dated" means it is:
questions.passage:
The film Lawrence of Arabia may be somewhat dated by modern special-effects standards, but it remains a high-water mark in the annals of filmmaking. Since we're on the subject of computer-generated special effects, Lawrence has none for the simple reason that it was actually filmed using real people who really performed the action. The long, slow scenes of camels walking in the desert may seem dull to the modern animation-jaded viewer, but those willing to pay attention to the underlying themes will be well rewarded by what the movie is saying.

And it is the theme of Lawrence that really stays with a viewer, even today, some 40 years after it was released. That theme is the age-old story of hubris, the pride of a man which raises him above his peers, only to dash him on the rocks of self-indulgence by the end.

We see this tragic foible of mankind worked out fully in the character of Lawrence himself, who begins the film as a brilliant genius who is eager to get involved in the desert conflicts of World War I. His cocky attitude irritates his superiors and amuses the Arabs fighting alongside the British, but his genius for details and strategy soon overcome all obstacles. Lawrence's brilliant victory at the Suez Canal could only have been accomplished by Lawrence, and his own self-sacrifice and commitment to his followers display the best of his character.

Unfortunately, the baser elements of that character gradually take control as the film moves along, and Lawrence slowly declines into a dangerous blend of despair and self-assurance. The problem gradually becomes evident: Lawrence has grown to believe that he is equal to God, the one who both gives life and takes it away again.

questions.text: Seven persons named P, Q, R, S, T, U and V are sitting in a straight line facing north. P is second to the left of V. U is third to the right of T. S is second to the left of T. Only S is sitting between R and P. Who is sitting second to the left of Q?
In a code language, 'PRIMEX' is written as 'YFNJSQ'. How will 'NECTAR' be written in that language?

Among six friends Chirag, Maya, Naitik, Parag, Saniya and Zena, Chirag scores lesser marks than Naitik. Saniya scores more marks than Naitik. Parag scores lesser marks than Zena. Zena scores lesser marks than Maya. Parag scores more marks than Saniya. Who scored the least marks among the six friends?

CAMP : BDNQ :: PLAN : BMOQ :: ROSE : ?

27, 52, 88, 137, ?, 282

Pointing to the photograph of a boy, Pratyush said, 'he is my father's only brother's wife's son'. How is Pratyush related to that boy's father's father?

metadata.notes: Rounding decimals to nearest integers first.

questions.text: What will be the approximate value of the following equation?
A man is standing in a park facing south direction, then he turns 90 degrees clockwise. After that he turns 135 degrees clockwise. In which direction is he facing now?

metadata.notes: Trial and error of swapping numbers.

questions.text:
Which two numbers should be interchanged to make the following equation correct?
16 − 42 + 24 × 12 ÷ 6 = 14

Select the combination of letters that when sequentially placed in the blanks of the given series will complete the series.
H _ B V _ W _ N _ V Z _ H _ B V _ W

options.text: N Z H B W N W
N Z H B W N Z
N Z H V W N Z
V Z H B W N Z

metadata.notes: Trick: Reverse alphabet numbering or sum of positional values.

questions.text: In a code language, 'CLASSY' is written as '79'. How will 'NIGHTS' be written in that language?

Each of K, L, M, N, O, P and R has an exam on a different day of the week starting on Monday and ending on Sunday of the same week. N's exam is on the day just after L's day of the exam. Only three persons have exams before P. K's exam is just before the day of M's exam. R's exam is on Wednesday. Only two persons take exams between R and K. Who has an exam on Friday?

(Left)
D H B 6 4 T Y U P K L A E 9 8 H B N 3 C X 2 B M 7 6 H K P A 6 4 3 E U P L 6 5 3 G V 4 2 K 9 A W
(Right)

If all the even numbers in the above string are deleted, then which would be the 10th letter/number from the right end in the remaining string?

metadata.answerKeyStatus: missing in source; official key skipped this figure question

questions.text: (Figure series question)

Read the given statements and conclusions carefully. Assuming that the information given in the statements is true, even if it appears to be at variance with commonly known facts, decide which conclusion(s) logically follow(s) from the statements.
Statements:

All utensils are buckets.
All spoons are buckets.
All buckets are dustbins.

Conclusions:

I. Some dustbins are utensils.
II. All spoons are dustbins.
III. No utensil is a dustbin.

options.text: All of I, II and III follow.

metadata.notes: Complex pattern combining multiplication and addition.

12, 14, 31, 97, ?, 1971

Six friends named P, Q, R, S, T and U are sitting around a circular table facing the centre but not necessarily in the same order. T is second to the left of S. U is third to the right of P. R is to the immediate right of U. Who is sitting second to the right of P?

metadata.notes: Decoding symbols to build a family tree.

questions.text:
A & B means 'A is the daughter of B'
A % B means 'A is the father of B'

A @ B means 'A is the sister of B'

A # B means 'A is the mother of B'

A + B means 'A is the husband of B'

If M @ R & N & C + H # F % K, then how is R related to F?

questions.text: (Figure / Mirror image question)
Teesta Barrage Project is one of the largest irrigation projects that was started in which of the following states?
In which of the following months of 2022 did the SENSEX Stock Market Index reach an all-time high?
Who headed the Indian National Congress when the Lucknow Pact was adopted in 1916?
In the month of December 2022, which of the following Fintech Unicorn companies was separated from Flipkart to operate under the US-based retail chain Walmart?
Which of the following languages has NOT been given the status of classical language in India?
Which of the following neighbouring countries of India has the highest population of Hindus?
Who among the following sportspersons became the first Indian to win a gold medal in the men's triple jump in Commonwealth Games 2022?
In the Indian Constitution under Right to Freedom (Articles 19 to 22), which of the following freedoms is NOT guaranteed to the citizens?

options.text: Protection in respect of conviction for offences
Freedom of conscience and free profession, practice and propagation of religion
Protection of life and personal liberty
Protection of certain rights regarding freedom of speech, etc.

metadata.notes: Biographical timeline of a historical figure.

questions.text: When did Subhash Chandra Bose quit his high-paying ICS job in England to join India's struggle for Independence?

Intranasal vaccine, which got clearance in the month of December 2022 as a booster option in the COVID-19 immunisation is produced by which of the following pharmaceutical companies?
The Zuari River is the longest river of which of the following states of India?
Who among the following Afghan rulers ruled in an interlude in the history of the Mughal Empire?
Which of the following festivals of Odisha state commemorates the 2,000-year-old maritime and cultural links between ancient Kalinga and Southeast Asia?
"The Accidental Prime Minister: The Making and Unmaking of Manmohan Singh" book was written by whom among the following authors?
As of January 2023, as per the Reserve Bank of India, which of the following is NOT a Scheduled Public Sector Bank anymore?

(ii) A compiler is a system software used to translate a high-level language program into its equivalent machine language program.
